articles.title: Spring 2026 Tornado Preparedness Guide for Homeowners
articles.summary: Learn how to prepare your home and family for peak tornado season in 2026, including choosing a certified shelter and building an emergency kit.
articles.body:
Tornado season in 2026 is expected to be active across the South and Midwest. Preparing your home now can significantly reduce risk for your family.

This guide covers:
- How to identify the safest areas in your home
- When a safe room or shelter is strongly recommended
- The difference between above-ground and underground shelters
- Essentials to keep in a 72-hour emergency kit

In the **Recommended Shelters** section, we highlight a mix of underground and above-ground options that meet FEMA and ICC-500 guidelines and are suitable for typical residential lots and garages.

articles.title: Family Tornado Safety Checklist: 15 Minutes to a Safer Home
articles.summary: A step-by-step checklist you can complete in under 15 minutes to improve tornado safety for your household.
articles.body:
Use this quick checklist to walk through your home and identify hazards before tornado season. Confirm where you will take shelter, how you will receive warnings, and what supplies you have on hand.

The article includes:
- Printable checklist for each room
- Tips for including children in emergency planning
- Guidance on when to upgrade to a safe room or storm shelter

In the **Recommended Shelters** section we suggest compact, budget-friendly models that fit in typical interior rooms or garages.

articles.title: Understanding Tornado Shelter Standards: FEMA P-361 and ICC-500
articles.summary: An overview of the key safety standards for storm shelters and why they matter when you choose a safe room.
articles.body:
Not all storm shelters are created equal. FEMA P-361 and ICC-500 provide rigorous design and testing criteria to ensure that safe rooms offer life-safety protection in extreme winds.

In this article, we break down the terminology, explain test procedures, and outline what certifications to look for when evaluating a shelter. The **Recommended Shelters** section highlights models that have been tested to these standards and are appropriate for typical single-family residences.

articles.title: Building the Ultimate Emergency Kit for Severe Weather
articles.summary: Lighting, first aid, and essential supplies to keep in or near your safe room for tornadoes and severe storms.
articles.body:
A well-stocked emergency kit is the perfect companion to a certified safe room. At minimum, plan for 72 hours of self-sufficiency for every member of your household.

This guide covers:
- Water, food, and medication planning
- Reliable lighting options that don’t depend on wall power
- First-aid essentials and trauma supplies

In the **Recommended Gear** section, we list affordable, high-rated lighting and first-aid products that fit neatly on a shelf inside your safe room.

articles.title: Garage Storm Shelter Buying Guide
articles.summary: How to choose between in-floor and above-floor garage storm shelters, with installation and cost considerations.
articles.body:
Garage storm shelters are a popular choice in regions with slab-on-grade construction. This guide compares in-floor (flush-mount) units with above-floor steel boxes that can double as storage platforms.

We discuss:
- Pros and cons of different garage shelter types
- Drainage and water intrusion considerations
- Typical installation timelines and permitting

In the **Recommended Garage Shelters** section, we feature models suited for 2-car garages and standard ceiling heights.

articles.title: Financing Your Residential Safe Room or Shelter
articles.summary: Explore payment options, financing terms, and cost ranges for residential safe rooms and small community shelters.
articles.body:
A code-compliant safe room is a significant investment, but financing can make monthly payments manageable. This article walks through:
- Typical price ranges for different shelter sizes
- How financing calculators estimate monthly payments
- What information lenders may require

In the **Sample Solutions** section, we reference mid-range residential and small commercial models to illustrate pricing and payment scenarios.

articles.title: Planning a Commercial Safe Room for 15–25 Employees
articles.summary: Key considerations for businesses designing shelters for small teams, including occupancy, accessibility, and financing.
articles.body:
Small offices and retail locations often need shelter space for 15–25 occupants. This article covers:
- How to determine required square footage
- Structural and accessibility requirements
- Budgeting and phased implementation

Our **Recommended Commercial Solutions** section highlights modular safe rooms designed for indoor installation in existing buildings.

articles.title: Indoor vs. Outdoor Safe Rooms: Which Is Right for Your Property?
articles.summary: Compare indoor safe rooms installed in garages or interior rooms with standalone outdoor shelters.
articles.body:
Choosing between an indoor safe room and an outdoor shelter depends on your lot, budget, and mobility needs.

This article compares:
- Access during severe weather
- Foundation and drainage requirements
- Long-term maintenance and resale impact

We also provide several example configurations and list a few compact, budget-friendly options in the **Recommended Models** section.

articles.title: Retrofitting Existing Basements for Safe Room Installation
articles.summary: Structural, moisture, and access considerations when adding a safe room to an existing basement.
articles.body:
Basements can be excellent locations for safe rooms, but retrofits require careful planning. We explain:
- How to evaluate existing foundation walls
- Dealing with moisture and sump systems
- Coordinating with local installers and permit offices

We include example floor plans and discuss several modular safe room sizes suitable for basements.

articles.title: Regional Severe Weather Risk Assessment (Draft)
articles.summary: Working draft summarizing regional tornado and wind risk patterns for future publication.
articles.body: This draft report is used internally and is not yet finalized for public guidance. Content is subject to change.
